title: Cross-reference closure and legal applicability graph (ZR 11-25 suffix inheritance; per-rule closure manifests; consumes ONLY eligible verified evidence)
objective: PROPOSED — NOT CONTRACTED (owner approval required). RENUMBERED from the former closure packet (owner directive 2026-07-23 five-packet split) because closure must now also depend on the accepted Document Evidence Verification Engine. Build a deterministic directed graph over the VERIFIED corpus covering: linked AND unlinked textual section citations; definitions imported from §12-10 and other scoped definition sections; §11-25 suffix inheritance; 'except as otherwise provided'; general-to-special priority; exceptions/modifications; special-purpose district overrides; appendices/mapped areas; tables and footnotes; exception language; amendment/supersession links; and citation-like text that could not be parsed. Every releasable rule family gets a closure manifest (entry sections; reachable controlling sections; imported definitions; applicability predicates; exceptions; overrides; UNRESOLVED reference/citation candidates; required property facts; source version/hash per node). A rule may proceed ONLY if all reachable references in its declared scope are resolved; otherwise a typed unsupported/professional_review_required result. It may CONSUME ONLY evidence spans eligible for rule drafting (M3-T003) and MUST REJECT: ocr_draft, extraction conflicts, unresolved table structures, unresolved citations, unconfirmed critical OCR values, and AI-proposed-but-unvalidated relationships. Unresolved citation candidates appear in the closure manifest and BLOCK a final rule value; unresolved reference is NEVER non-applicable; single-chapter search cannot satisfy an absence assertion; AI-extracted edges are PROPOSED until deterministic evidence or required human review validates them; cycles terminate deterministically; renumbered/deleted sections detected. M4-T007+ depends on accepted M3-T004. Full spec: project-control/reports/M3-CORPUS-REPLAN-PROPOSAL.md.
business_reason: Completeness must be proven by reachable-reference closure over VERIFIED evidence, not assembled from one chapter and not from unverified OCR. §11-25 suffix inheritance and §23-422's express exclusion must be modeled before any bare-R5 applicability or absence claim, and before the downstream M4 yard/coverage slices are re-scoped. Gating closure on eligible-evidence-only is what keeps an unverified critical value out of every rule.
inputs: M3-T002 accepted AS PRODUCTION CORPUS: per-section raw+normalized hashes, cross-reference hrefs, definitions, last-amended/effective dates, amendment/supersession, tables/footnotes

M3-T003 accepted: the evidence-state machine + eligible evidence spans (native-extracted+replayed+cross-source-reconciled OR human-confirmed OCR); the closure graph consumes ONLY eligible spans and reads the evidence contracts (evidence_span, cross_source_comparison) read-only

M3-T001 accepted: LEGAL_CORPUS_COVERAGE_MATRIX.md, legal_source_manifest schema, DOCUMENT_EVIDENCE_POLICY.md (integration boundary: closure never reads raw OCR strings), authoritative status vocabulary
ZR §11-25 (suffix inheritance unless expressly separate), §23-422 express exclusion 'except R5 Districts with a letter suffix', §12-10 definitions
services/api/app/rules/** (M4-T001 engine): coverage vocabulary, rule_conflict/competing-rules trace, unsupported/professional_review_required outcomes — consume unchanged
outputs: services/api/app/corpus/closure/** — graph builder, linked+unlinked+table/footnote citation extractor, §11-25 suffix-inheritance resolver, closure-manifest generator, reachability + deterministic cycle termination, renumber/delete detector, proposed-vs-validated edge state, eligible-evidence gate (rejects ocr_draft/conflict/unresolved/unconfirmed/AI-unvalidated) (OWNED)

packages/contracts/schemas/v1/closure_manifest.schema.json — NEW versioned internal schema (deterministic $id+version; entry sections, reachable sections, imported definitions, applicability predicates, exceptions, overrides, unresolved candidates, required facts, per-node version/hash, per-node evidence-eligibility reference)

packages/contracts/schemas/v1/fixtures/closure_manifest/{positive,negative}/*.json — positive validates; negatives missing the unresolved-reference field or per-node source-version/hash or evidence-eligibility reference fail

services/api/tests/corpus/closure/** — deterministic cross-reference-closure harness incl. adversarial unlinked-reference/table-footnote 'cannot be silently missed' + ineligible-evidence-rejection tests

services/api/tests/fixtures/corpus/closure/** — bounded frozen fixtures (§23-361/R5B inheritance, §23-422 suffix exclusion, cyclic ref, renumbered/deleted section, unresolved definition, unlinked textual citation, table footnote citation, ocr_draft span, extraction_conflict span)
forbidden_paths: services/api/app/corpus/ingest/**, storage/**, versioning/** (M3-T002), extractors/**, evidence/**, document_validation/** (M3-T003), construction_code/** and overlay/** (M3-T005) — import read-only, never mutate
Reading raw OCR strings or ingesting ocr_draft/conflicted/unconfirmed/AI-unvalidated evidence into a closure (integration boundary); editing the rules evaluator core or any ruleset

Publishing/Verifying any rule; emitting a final value when any reachable reference/citation candidate is unresolved; treating an unresolved reference as non-applicable; treating an AI-extracted edge as validated without deterministic evidence or human review

Asserting 'confirmed absent' from a single-chapter scan; editing canonical contracts; apps/web/**; project-control/** except own reports; .claude/** except own agent-memory; adding any new runtime dependency (stdlib graph)
acceptance_scenarios: AS-1 suffix inheritance: §23-361 applied to R5B imports §11-25 inheritance unless the section expressly provides a different suffix rule -> R5B inherits R5 provisions where no separate R5B provision exists.
AS-2 express exclusion wins: §23-422's express 'except R5 Districts with a letter suffix' prevents R5 values leaking to R5A/R5B/R5D; the closure records the express exclusion, not inheritance.
AS-3 unresolved blocks value: an unresolved definition/exception/citation candidate in a rule's reachable set -> NO final value (typed unsupported/professional_review_required).
AS-4 cycle termination: a reference cycle terminates deterministically (no infinite loop, no hidden cutoff).
AS-5 renumber/delete detection: a renumbered/deleted referenced section is detected and flagged unresolved, not silently followed to a stale node.

AS-6 manifest completeness: each releasable rule family's closure manifest lists entry sections, reachable controlling sections, imported definitions, applicability predicates, exceptions, overrides, unresolved candidates, required property facts, per-node version/hash, and the per-node evidence-eligibility reference.
AS-7 determinism: identical corpus version + evidence set -> byte-identical closure manifest + graph.

AS-8 unlinked + table/footnote citations: closure processes linked AND unlinked textual citations, definition imports, tables/footnotes, exception language, amendment links, and unparseable citation-like text; adversarial tests prove an unlinked reference or table-footnote citation CANNOT be silently missed.

AS-9 eligible-evidence gate (NEW): closure REJECTS ocr_draft, extraction_conflict, unresolved table structures, unresolved citations, unconfirmed critical OCR values, and AI-proposed-but-unvalidated relationships; a fixture carrying any of these produces no final value and is surfaced, not consumed.

AS-10 proposed-edge discipline: an AI-extracted edge is PROPOSED and cannot contribute to a resolved closure (or a final rule value) until deterministic evidence or required human review validates it.

AS-11 closure_manifest schema validation (executable): schema meta-validates; positive fixture validates; negatives FAIL including one missing the unresolved-reference/citation-candidate field, one missing per-node source-version/hash, and one missing the evidence-eligibility reference; deterministic $id + version.
NC-1 single-chapter absence: a search restricted to one chapter cannot satisfy an absence assertion (rejected as unsupported).
NC-2 unresolved != non-applicable: a reference/citation the graph cannot resolve is never coerced to not_applicable or a default value.

NC-3 bare-R5 not auto-ambiguous: bare R5 in a section with no separate suffix provisions is modeled via §11-25 inheritance; a section expressly listing distinct R5A/R5B/R5D provisions lets those express provisions control.
risks: PROPOSAL ONLY: no dispatch until owner approves the packet + dependency order.
DEPENDS ON PRODUCTION M3-T002 + accepted M3-T003 evidence engine: closure consumes ONLY eligible verified evidence; a fixture-only M3-T002 or an un-accepted M3-T003 does not satisfy this dependency.
GATES M4: M4-T007 and every later yard/coverage/rear/front/side rule DEPENDS ON accepted M3-T004; the slice boundaries are regenerated from this closure set.

SILENT-MISS + INELIGIBLE-EVIDENCE HAZARD: unlinked citations, table footnotes, unparseable citation-like text, and any ocr_draft/conflicted/unconfirmed span are the highest-risk gaps — they must surface and block a final value, never be dropped or consumed.
LEGAL BOUNDARY: closure resolves references structurally; where resolution needs legal interpretation, the result is professional_review_required, never a guessed edge.
requirement_refs: project-control/reports/M3-CORPUS-REPLAN-PROPOSAL.md — full spec (owner directive 2026-07-23 five-packet split; §11-25 correction; §15 integration boundary)
docs/DOCUMENT_EVIDENCE_POLICY.md (integration boundary — closure never reads raw OCR)
harness_assignments: CROSS-REFERENCE-CLOSURE HARNESS: §23-361/R5B imports §11-25 unless expressly overridden; §23-422 express exclusion; unresolved definition/exception/citation-candidate -> no final value; cycles terminate; renumber/delete detected; single-chapter cannot satisfy absence; linked+unlinked+table/footnote+unparseable citations cannot be silently missed; AI edges proposed until validated.
ELIGIBLE-EVIDENCE-GATE HARNESS (NEW): ocr_draft / extraction_conflict / unresolved-table / unresolved-citation / unconfirmed-critical-OCR / AI-unvalidated spans are rejected, never consumed.
LOT/APPLICABILITY + RULE-BEHAVIOR + ARCHITECT-BENCHMARK harnesses remain DOWNSTREAM (M4 yard/coverage slices consuming this closure).
